name: Red Hill Brewery
address: 88 Shoreham Rd, Red Hill South VIC 3937
signature: The Peninsula's original craft brewery — Belgian-style ales from the only estate hop farm in Victoria
editorNote:
Red Hill Brewery is the oldest serious craft brewery on the Peninsula and still one of the few in Australia that grows its own hops. That detail matters: the farm-to-glass story here is not marketing, it is infrastructure. Dave and Karen Golding planted the hop garden in 2005 and have been making Belgian-style ales from it ever since — witbier, dubbel, tripel, and the seasonal fresh-hop beers that are worth waiting for.

The cellar door is a low barn at the top of a vineyard track, with picnic tables, a wood-fired pizza oven, and a tasting flight that doubles as a civilised lunch. Bring the dog. Bring walking shoes for the hop garden loop afterwards.

A proper alternative to a winery lunch if your group includes someone who actively dislikes pinot noir.

heroImage.alt: Red Hill Brewery cellar door barn, hop garden, and picnic tables
